fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Washington CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Washington - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity washington, how to transfer CNA license to washington, washington CNA endorsement application

board.name: Washington DOH
board.mailing_address:
Washington State Department of Health
P.O. Box 47865
Olympia, WA 98504-7865

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Washington.

steps.title: Complete an Approved Training Program
steps.description: Successfully complete a Washington State Department of Health (DOH) approved Nursing Assistant training program, or demonstrate equivalent training and experience.

steps.title: Apply Online through the e-Service
steps.description: Create or log in to your account on the Washington State Licensing e-Service portal. Complete the online application for Nursing Assistant Certified (NA-C) and pay the required application fee.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload any necessary documents directly through the Washington State Licensing e-Service, such as proof of training program completion. Ensure all documentation is complete and accurate as specified by the DOH.

steps.title: Schedule and Pass NNAAP Exams
steps.description: After your application is approved, register and pass both the written/oral and skills portions of the National Nurse Aide Assessment Program (NNAAP) examination administered by Pearson VUE.

steps.title: Receive Your Credential
steps.description: Once all requirements, including passing the NNAAP exams, are met and processed by the Washington State Department of Health, your NA-C credential will be issued, and your name will be added to the Nurse Aide Registry.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: Who administers the Washington NAC exam?
faqs.answer: The WA Nursing Assistant Certified exam is administered by Credentia. Register at credentia.com/test-takers/wa.

faqs.question: How do I verify a Washington CNA/NAC?
faqs.answer: Use the WA DOH Provider Credential Search at fortress.wa.gov/doh/providercredentialsearch/. Search for credential type 'Nursing Assistant Certification – NC'.

reciprocity.notes: Washington uses the NAC Application Packet for endorsement. The endorsement section of the packet covers out-of-state transfers. An Authorization to Test may be required.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a NAC in WA.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Washington.

hero.title: Become a NAC in Washington
hero.dek: To apply for your Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) license in Washington, you must create an account and submit your application through the Washington State Licensing e-Service portal.

seo.title: How to Become a NAC in Washington
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Washington NAC certification.

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to the Washington State Licensing e-Service portal.
2. Click 'Register' to create a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account, or 'Login' if you already have one.
3. Follow the prompts to set up your SAW account and link it to the DOH Professional Licensing Services.
4. Once logged in, select the application for 'Nursing Assistant Certified (NA-C)' to begin.
